title: Systems Engineer
description: Build high-performance, low-level software that forms the bedrock of modern computing infrastructure.

courses.title: The C Programming Language (K&R)
courses.description: The authoritative reference by Brian W. Kernighan and Dennis M. Ritchie.

courses.title: Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective
courses.description: CS:APP by Randal E. Bryant and David R. O'Hallaron. Crucial for understanding how hardware impacts software.

courses.title: Systems Performance
courses.description: Enterprise and the Cloud by Brendan Gregg.

details.importance: They build the lower levels of the tech stack—compilers, OS kernels, database engines, and browsers—that all other software fundamentally relies on.
details.rolesAndResponsibilities: Writing low-level, highly optimized C/C++/Rust code, managing memory manually, and tuning hardware interactions.
details.aiImpact: Moderate. AI can optimize specific algorithms, but low-level hardware orchestration and legacy system maintenance remain human-dominated.
details.marketDemand: Steady. Not as explosive as web development, but completely vital for infrastructure giants.

expectations.junior: Write device drivers, optimize specific algorithms, and fix memory leaks.
expectations.mid: Design concurrent systems, architect embedded software, and tune deep performance bottlenecks.
expectations.senior: Architect entirely new file systems, maintain complex standard libraries, and write programming language compilers.
